data.name: CreatingDumpFile
data.value: Creating dump file '{0}'
data.comment: {0} is the dump file path.

data.name: DumpingProcess
data.value: Dumping process {0} - {1}
data.comment: 0 is pid, 1 is the name of the process

data.name: DumpingProcessTree
data.value: Dumping this process tree (from bottom):

data.name: ErrorGettingParentOfProcess
data.value: Error getting parent of process {0} - {1}: {2}"
data.comment: 0 is pid, 1 is name, 2 is the exception

data.name: ErrorKillingProcess
data.value: Error while killing process {0} - {1}: {2}
data.comment: 0 is pid, 1 is name, 2 is the exception

data.name: ErrorWhileDumpingProcess
data.value: Error while taking dump of process {0} - {1}: {2}
data.comment: 0 is pid, 1 is name, 2 is the exception from the system

data.name: HangDumpArtifactDescription
data.value: The hang dump file

data.name: HangDumpArtifactDisplayName
data.value: Hang dump file

data.name: HangDumpEnvironmentVariableInvalidValueErrorMessage
data.value: Environment variable '{0}' is set to '{1}' instead of '{2}'
data.comment: {0} is the environment variable name. {1} is the actual value. {2} is the expected value.

data.name: HangDumpEnvironmentVariableIsMissingErrorMessage
data.value: Environment variable '{0}' is not set
data.comment: {0} is the environment variable name.

data.name: HangDumpExtensionDescription
data.value: Produce hang dump files when a test execution exceed a given time.

data.name: HangDumpExtensionDisplayName
data.value: Hang dump

data.name: HangDumpFailed
data.comment: {0} is the exception that occurred. {1} is disk information detailing free space.

data.name: HangDumpFileNameOptionDescription
data.value: Specify the name of the dump file. Supports the following placeholders: {pname} (test application name), {pid} (process ID), {asm} (entry assembly name), {tfm} (target framework moniker), {arch} (process architecture), {time} (timestamp). The legacy %p token (process ID) is also supported for backward compatibility.
data.comment: Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump-filename"}, placeholder tokens {Locked="{pname}"}{Locked="{pid}"}{Locked="{asm}"}{Locked="{tfm}"}{Locked="{arch}"}{Locked="{time}"}, or legacy token {Locked="%p"}.

data.name: HangDumpOptionDescription
data.value: Generate a dump file if the test process hangs
data.comment: Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump"}.

data.name: HangDumpTimeoutExpired
data.value: Hang dump timeout of '{0}' expired
data.comment: {0} is the hang dump timeout value.

data.name: HangDumpTimeoutOptionDescription
data.value: Specify the timeout after which the dump will be generated. The timeout value is specified in one of the following formats: 500ms, 500mil, 500millisecond, 500milliseconds, 5400s, 5400sec, 5400second, 5400seconds, 90m, 90min, 90minute, 90minutes, 1.5h, 1.5hour, 1.5hours, 1d, 1day, 1days. A bare number (with no suffix) is interpreted as milliseconds. Default is 30m.
data.comment: Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump-timeout"} or time examples {Locked="500ms"}{Locked="500mil"}{Locked="500millisecond"}{Locked="500milliseconds"}{Locked="5400s"}{Locked="5400sec"}{Locked="5400second"}{Locked="5400seconds"}{Locked="90m"}{Locked="90min"}{Locked="90minute"}{Locked="90minutes"}{Locked="1.5h"}{Locked="1.5hour"}{Locked="1.5hours"}{Locked="1d"}{Locked="1day"}{Locked="1days"}{Locked="30m"}.

data.name: HangDumpTimeoutOptionInvalidArgument
data.value: '--hangdump-timeout' expects a single timeout argument
data.comment: Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump-timeout"}.

data.name: HangDumpTypeAndIfSupportedAreMutuallyExclusiveErrorMessage
data.value: '--hangdump-type' and '--hangdump-type-if-supported' are mutually exclusive. Use '--hangdump-type-if-supported' to opt into a best-effort dump type that falls back to the closest available type (with an informational message) when the requested type is unavailable on the current runtime, or '--hangdump-type' to fail fast on unsupported runtimes.
data.comment: Do not localize option names {Locked="--hangdump-type"}{Locked="--hangdump-type-if-supported"}.

data.name: HangDumpTypeIfSupportedFallbackInfoMessage
data.value: '--hangdump-type-if-supported {0}' is not supported on the current runtime. Falling back to '{1}'.
data.comment: 0 is the requested dump type. 1 is the fallback dump type. Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump-type-if-supported"}.

data.name: HangDumpTypeIfSupportedOptionDescription
data.value: Same as '--hangdump-type' but silently falls back (with an informational message) to the closest supported dump type when the requested type is not available on the current runtime (e.g. 'Triage' is only supported on .NET Core and falls back to 'Mini' on .NET Framework). Use this option to keep the same command line across CI matrices that mix .NET Framework and .NET. Valid values are 'Mini', 'Heap', 'Full', 'Triage', 'None'. Mutually exclusive with '--hangdump-type'.
data.comment: Do not localize option names {Locked="--hangdump-type"}{Locked="--hangdump-type-if-supported"}, dump type values {Locked="Mini"}{Locked="Heap"}{Locked="Full"}{Locked="Triage"}{Locked="None"}, or runtime names {Locked=".NET Core"}{Locked=".NET Framework"}{Locked=".NET"}.

data.name: HangDumpTypeOptionDescription
data.value: Specify the type of the dump. Valid values are {0}. Default type is 'Full'
data.comment: 0 is the list of valid dump type values. Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump-type"} or default dump type {Locked="Full"}.

data.name: HangDumpTypeOptionInvalidType
data.value: '{0}' is not a valid dump type. Valid options are {1}
data.comment: {0} is the invalid dump type. {1} is the list of valid dump type values.

data.name: HangDumpUnsupportedRequestTypeErrorMessage
data.value: Request of type '{0}' is not supported
data.comment: {0} is the unsupported request type name.

data.name: HangTestListArtifactDescription
data.value: The list of tests that were running at the time of the hang

data.name: HangTestListArtifactDisplayName
data.value: Hang test list

data.name: MissingHangDumpMainOption
data.value: You specified one or more hang dump parameters but did not enable it, add --hangdump to the command line
data.comment: Do not localize option name {Locked="--hangdump"}.

data.name: RunningTestsWhileDumping
data.value: The following tests were still running when dump was taken (format: [<time-elapsed-since-start>] <name>):
data.comment: Do not localize format tokens {Locked="[<time-elapsed-since-start>]"} and {Locked="<name>"}.
